stages.purpose: Search both name-and-location pairs in the same assistant action; all inputs are known and neither search depends on the other.
Only after stage 1 returns one match for a branch, use that returned ID in a later assistant action; when both branches resolve, the two gets are independent and belong together.

state_assertions: No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
cal-981 and cal-881 remain untouched distractors.
Each get ID equals the sole match returned by its own search; no ID is inferred from the prompt or fixture.
response_constraints: Base the comparison only on successful get responses, not on abbreviated search matches.
For a branch with zero or multiple search matches, omit its get; still complete any independent branch with exactly one match and identify the unresolved branch.
Do not expose distractor records or propose or perform changes.
Return only a two-row Markdown table with columns Record, ID, Date, and Status, preserving the requested order.

prompt: The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale matches. Find the meeting named “Winter Operations Drill” at “Salt Lake Depot” and the meeting named “Continuity Tabletop” at “Phoenix Operations Center”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Return only a two-row Markdown table with columns Record, ID, Date, and Status, preserving the requested order.
